subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze Albert Einstein’s life, thought processes, and personality in depth
Evaluate the challenges he faced and the strategies he used to overcome them
Identify and discuss literary devices such as narrative technique, imagery, and characterization
Draw inferences about creativity, intelligence, and perseverance
Connect lessons from Einstein’s life to social, educational, and personal contexts
Develop advanced critical thinking, comprehension, and analytical writing skills

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Contextual Analysis
topics_covered: Author: Sudha Murthy and her narrative style
Historical, social, and educational context of Einstein’s life
Significance of curiosity and innovation in science
teaching_strategy: Board diagram showing early life, schooling, and context
student_activities: Predict key challenges and achievements based on introduction
Discuss the role of curiosity and creativity in personal growth

lesson_plan.title: Close Reading – Early Life and Character Analysis
topics_covered: Einstein’s childhood, schooling, and personality traits
Psychological and emotional factors affecting learning
Early signs of curiosity and independent thinking
teaching_strategy: Guided analysis of text to identify traits and motivations
student_activities: Extract textual evidence supporting traits like curiosity and patience
Explain how challenges shaped character and thinking

lesson_plan.title: Critical Reading – Achievements and Problem-Solving
topics_covered: Strategies to overcome societal and academic obstacles
teaching_strategy: Discussion of cause-and-effect between challenges and achievements
Board illustration of timeline of achievements
student_activities: Analyze how challenges contributed to personal growth

lesson_plan.title: Themes, Literary Devices, and Analytical Thinking
topics_covered: Themes: Curiosity, perseverance, creativity, and intelligence
Literary devices: imagery, narrative style, and characterization
Critical thinking on moral and educational lessons from the text
teaching_strategy: Group discussion and brainstorming on themes and lessons
student_activities: Identify literary devices and their effect on reader understanding
Discuss how the author highlights intelligence and curiosity

lesson_plan.title: Critical Summary and Inference
topics_covered: Writing a detailed summary with inferred insights
Connecting Einstein’s qualities to personal, educational, and social life
teaching_strategy: Timeline chart for events and achievements
Guided discussion on inference-making and critical summary
student_activities: Create a detailed timeline of Einstein’s life and work
Write an 8–10 sentence summary with critical analysis and inference
assessment: Quality and depth of summary and inferences

lesson_plan.title: Vocabulary, Reflection, and Application
topics_covered: Reflective writing on lessons learned from Einstein’s life
Application of story insights in personal and academic goals
teaching_strategy: Board explanation of advanced vocabulary with examples
Reflective prompts and discussion for deeper connection
student_activities: List new words with meanings and use in sentences
Write a reflective paragraph analyzing personal growth inspired by Einstein
Group discussion on how to cultivate curiosity, creativity, and perseverance

teacher_tip: Encourage students to critically analyze Einstein’s thought process, evaluate his problem-solving methods, and connect the story’s lessons to personal, educational, and social contexts.
